message.reasoning_content:
We are asked: "You split a short research text into its distinct FACETS - the separate aspects, conditions or sub-claims it actually contains (the method, its scope/condition, a result, a limitation). Output ONLY a JSON array of 2-4 short strings, each a self-contained facet in the text's own terms - no invention, no overlap. If the text has only one facet, return one element."

The text: "Cold Phase-Space Limits on SPARC-Calibrated Stochastic-Drift Phenomenology: Reduced Data and Reproducibility Package. This package provides reduced numerical tables, figure products, validation summaries, configuration records, and reusable scripts for a computational study of cold phase-space limits on SPARC-calibrated stochastic-drift phenomenology. The archive supports reproduction of the quoted headline values and reuse of the stochastic-response calculation products. It includes SPARC drift-calibration summaries, stochastic-field normalization checks, disc-heating screens, orbit-stream production-grid out"

We need to extract facets: separate aspects, conditions, sub-claims. The text describes a data package. The facets might be: what it provides, what it supports, what it includes. But careful: "in the text's own terms" and no invention. So we should pick out distinct claims or aspects. The text says: "This package provides reduced numerical tables, figure products, validation summaries, configuration records, and reusable scripts for a computational study..." That's one facet: the package provides certain data/scripts. Another facet: "The archive supports reproduction of the quoted headline values and reuse of the stochastic-response calculation products." That's a separate claim: it supports reproduction and reuse. A third facet: "It includes SPARC drift-calibration summaries, stochastic-field normalization checks, disc-heating screens, orbit-stream production-grid out" (likely cut off). So that's what it includes. So we have three facets: (1) provision of reduced data products and scripts, (2) support for reproduction and reuse, (3) inclusion of specific items. But are these distinct and non-overlapping? They each convey a different aspect: what it provides, what it enables, what it contains. So we can output JSON array of three strings. Use original phrasing as much as possible. The first: "provides reduced numerical tables, figure products, validation summaries, configuration records, and reusable scripts" The second: "supports reproduction of the quoted headline values and reuse of the stochastic-response calculation products" The third: "includes SPARC drift-calibration summaries, stochastic-field normalization checks, disc-heating screens, orbit-stream production-grid out" The last might be incomplete, but we should use it as is. So output: ["provides reduced numerical tables, figure products, validation summaries, configuration records, and reusable scripts", "supports reproduction of the quoted headline values and reuse of the stochastic-response calculation products", "includes SPARC drift-calibration summaries, stochastic-field normalization checks, disc-heating screens, orbit-stream production-grid out"] But check if there is any condition or limitation? The first part "Cold Phase-Space Limits on SPARC-Calibrated Stochastic-Drift Phenomenology: Reduced Data and Reproducibility Package." is likely a title, not a facet. So we ignore the title. So indeed, three facets.
